title: BOM Decomposition — NAICS 488310 Port and Harbor Operations

sections.title: NAICS Routing Note
sections.content:
NAICS 488310 is **Transportation & Warehousing (service sector)**. The port's own "output" is cargo throughput — an outcome, not a physical product, so the port itself has no Product BOM. The BOM below is for **Mein Solar's deliverable** (the commissioned BESS installation), which IS a physical product installed at port sites. Port-specific adaptations (salt-air IP66 hardening, shore-power frequency conversion interface, crane regen-braking buffer sizing) are marked where they diverge from the standard BESS BOM.

---

sections.title: L5 — End Product (Mein Solar position)
sections.content:
```
L5  Large-Scale BESS Installation (port-hardened, 1–10 MWh)  ← [MEIN SOLAR] position
    | EPC-delivered, VDE-AR-N 4110 certified, commissioned asset
```

The focal company occupies **L5**: the entire BOM below represents the composition of Mein Solar's deliverable. Per §02f, Mein Solar is an L1 EPC Integrator (VN tier) delivering a commissioned L0 asset (VN tier); in BOM terms that asset sits at L5 and is decomposed below.

---

sections.title: L4 Subsystems (per B.1)
sections.content:
| # | L4 Subsystem | Function | Port-specific note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 4.1 | Battery Energy Storage Subsystem | Store / release electrochemical energy | Sizing for crane regen + ferry peak pulses; LFP preferred for safety near fuel & vessels |
| 4.2 | Power Conversion Subsystem | DC↔AC bidirectional, 4-quadrant | Low-frequency output (60 Hz→50/60 Hz converter) for shore power via add-on stage |
| 4.3 | MV Grid Coupling Subsystem | Couple to 10/20 kV DSO + internal port MV ring | Ring feed to shore-power connection points; OPS IEC/IEEE 80005-1 interface |
| 4.4 | Energy Management & Control Subsystem | Dispatch, optimize, SCADA uplink | Integrated with port TOS (Terminal Operating System) for crane load forecast |
| 4.5 | Containment & Environmental Subsystem | Physical enclosure, climate, fire | **IP66 salt-air**, NEMA 4X doors, marine-grade coating |
| 4.6 | Civil Works Subsystem | Foundation, trenching, perimeter | Quay-side seismic/hydrostatic uplift; bollard protection for terminal tractor impact |

---

sections.title: B.2/B.3 — L4→L3→L2→L1→L0 Decomposition with Alternatives
sections.content:
### L4.1 — Battery Energy Storage Subsystem → L3 modules

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Cell + BMS + Thermal + Fire (integrated module architecture) | 95% | → Stable | HIGH | IDTechEx 2025 stationary storage |
| Discrete non-modular (loose rack + external BMS) | 5% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | Legacy pre-2020 installations |

#### L3.1.1 — Battery Pack / String Module → L2

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Rack-and-module (hot-swappable modules in 19″/600 mm rack) | 85% | → Stable | HIGH | CATL EnerC / Sungrow PowerTitan / BYD MC Cube spec sheets |
| Blade/Cell-to-Pack (CTP) architecture | 12% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | BYD CTP, CATL Tener (2024 launch) |
| Legacy pack-in-cabinet | 3% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | pre-2020 C&I installations |

##### L2.1.1.1 — Battery Cell Assembly → L1 parts

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Prismatic can cell | 78% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | BNEF 2025 stationary form-factor mix |
| Cylindrical (21700, 46xx) | 12% | → Stable | HIGH | Tesla Megapack |
| Pouch cell | 10% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | LG ESS, SDI legacy |

###### L1 — Prismatic Cell → L0 raw materials

| Alternative (cathode chemistry) | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **LFP (LiFePO₄)** | 58% of Li-ion BESS; >90% of new stationary in CN | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Wood Mackenzie 2025 |
| NMC (Li-Ni-Mn-Co-O) | 30% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | BNEF, IEA 2025 |
| NCA (Li-Ni-Co-Al-O) | 7% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | Tesla legacy stationary |
| LMFP / sodium-ion (emerging) | 5% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | CATL/HiNa 2024–25 pilots |

**port-relevance:** LFP preferred for fire-safety near moored vessels & fuel infrastructure.

| L0 commodity (LFP cathode) | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Lithium carbonate (battery-grade) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | CRU / Benchmark Mineral Intelligence — single-source:true |
| Iron phosphate precursor | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | industry standard — single-source:true |

| L0 commodity (anode) | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Natural graphite | 58% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | USGS 2025 |
| Synthetic graphite | 40% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Benchmark Mineral Intelligence |
| Silicon-carbon composite | 2% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | stationary not mainstream yet |

| L0 commodity (separator / electrolyte / current collectors) | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PE/PP separator | 95% | → Stable | HIGH | industry default |
| Ceramic-coated separator | 5% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | high-safety stationary trim |
| LiPF₆ in EC/DMC carbonate electrolyte | 98% | → Stable | HIGH | analyst standard |
| Aluminum foil (cathode collector) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | single-source:true |
| Copper foil (anode collector) | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | single-source:true |

##### L2.1.1.2 — Rack / Mechanical Assembly → L1 parts → L0

| L1 part | L0 commodity | Share | Trend | Conf. | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Steel rack frame | Cold-rolled low-carbon steel | 95% | → Stable | HIGH | single-source; stainless 5% for coastal |
| Busbar | Electrolytic copper (C10100/C11000) | 92% | → Stable | HIGH | aluminum busbar 8% cost-driven |
| Bolted connectors | Copper-tin plated | 100% | → Stable | HIGH | single-source:true |

#### L3.1.2 — BMS Module → L2

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Distributed (BMU per module + master BCU) | 82% | ↑ Growing | HIGH | Sungrow, CATL, BYD default |
| Centralized (single master, harness to cells) | 15% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | legacy C&I |
| Wireless BMS (Dukosi, ADI) | 3% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | pilot only |

##### L2 → L1 parts (BMU PCB, cell-voltage harness, current sensor, isolation monitor)

| L1 | L0 | Share | Conf. |
|---|---|---|---|
| Multilayer PCB | FR-4 glass-epoxy laminate + copper clad | 100% | HIGH (single-source) |
| Cell-voltage harness | PVC/XLPE wire + tin-plated copper | 100% | HIGH (single-source) |
| Current sensor | Hall-effect IC (95%) / shunt+amplifier (5%) | — | HIGH |
| Isolation monitor relay | Silicon IC + ceramic-isolated barrier | 100% | HIGH (single-source) |

#### L3.1.3 — Thermal Management Module → L2

| Alternative | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Liquid cooling (cold-plate / immersion return)** | 70% stationary containerized | ↑ Growing | HIGH | BIS Research 2025; IDTechEx |
| Forced-air (HVAC + fan array) | 28% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | C&I legacy |
| Immersion (two-phase dielectric) | 2% | ↑ Emerging | LOW | Cargill/3M pilots |

##### L2 → L1 → L0 (liquid cooling branch)

| L1 part | L0 commodity | Share | Trend | Conf. |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Cold plate | Aluminum 6061 extrusion | 90% | → Stable | HIGH |
| Coolant | Glycol-water (50/50 ethylene or propylene) | 95% | → Stable | HIGH |
| Chiller unit | R-1234yf refrigerant | 55% / R-513A 40% / R-32 5% | ↑/→/↓ | MEDIUM |
| Pump | Cast-iron centrifugal w/ 316 SS impeller | 100% | → Stable | HIGH (single-source) |
| Hose | EPDM rubber | 100% | → Stable | HIGH (single-source) |

#### L3.1.4 — Fire Suppression Module → L2 → L1 → L0

| Alternative (suppression agent) | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NOVEC 1230 / FK-5-1-12 (fluoroketone) | 55% | → Stable | HIGH | 3M / Chemours datasheets |
| Aerosol (pyrotechnic K₂CO₃) | 25% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | Stat-X, DSPA adoption |
| Water mist | 12% | ↑ Growing | MEDIUM | NFPA 855 shift |
| FM-200 (HFC-227ea) | 5% | ↓ Declining | HIGH | F-gas phase-down |
| Direct-injection CO₂ | 3% | ↓ Declining | MEDIUM | legacy |

| L1 part | L0 | Share | Conf. |
|---|---|---|---|
| Pressure cylinder | Seamless steel (4130) | 100% | HIGH (single-source) |
| Detection sensor | Si CMOS IC (gas) + NTC thermistor (heat) | 100% | HIGH (single-source) |
| Deflagration vent panel | Galvanized steel + rupture membrane | 100% | HIGH (single-source) |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| DC-link capacitor | Film capacitor (PP metallized) | Polypropylene film + aluminum | 95% | → Stable | HIGH |
| LCL filter | Ferrite / iron-powder core inductor + PP cap | Ferrite 70% / iron-powder 30% | — | → | HIGH |
| Cooling plate | Aluminum 6061 | 100% | → | HIGH (single-source) | — |

| L2 | L1 | L0 | Share | Conf. |
|---|---|---|---|---|

| L3 module | Share | Trend | Confidence | Source |

Software is non-physical; L0 terminates at L2 for pure software. Physical host below:

sections.title: B.5 — Company Position Marking
sections.content:
```
L5  Large-Scale BESS Installation (port-hardened)  ← [MEIN SOLAR] position — EPC integrator delivers full L5
    |
    +-- L4.1 Battery Energy Storage Subsystem         (sourced from CATL/Sungrow/SolarEast)
    +-- L4.2 Power Conversion Subsystem                (sourced from SMA/Sungrow/Power Electronics)
    +-- L4.3 MV Grid Coupling Subsystem                (sourced from Siemens/ABB/Schneider — ester-fluid trafo)
    +-- L4.4 EMS & Control Subsystem                   (integrated by Mein Solar; EMS via Entrix/Next Kraftwerke)
    +-- L4.5 Containment Subsystem                     (C5-M marine-grade — port-specific adaptation)
    +-- L4.6 Civil Works Subsystem                     (Mein Solar self-performs — core incumbent capability)
```

**Two-BOM observation (§B.5 rule):**
- **Downward product BOM** = above (Mein Solar's asset composition → drives supplier strategy).
- **Upward context BOM** = not applicable; Mein Solar is an L5 end-product deliverable at the customer's site, not a component inside a larger commercial product. The "larger system" is the port terminal itself — which has no industrial BOM (it's an operational aggregation, not a manufactured product).

---

sections.title: B.6 — Strategic BOM Adjacency (compact)
sections.content:
### Horizontal — Adjacent L4 systems Mein Solar could add to the asset

| Adjacent L4 | Capability Gap | Market Size (est.) | Strategic Fit |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Shore Power Conversion subsystem** (50↔60 Hz, 6.6/11 kV frequency converter) | MEDIUM — needs AFE + isolation transformer design | ~€2 bn EU ports to 2030 (EU AFIR mandate) | **STRONG** — adjacent to PCS domain |
| **HPC Charging subsystem** for port-truck / reach-stacker fleets | LOW — already in Mein Solar feature set | ~€500 M DE port logistics to 2030 | **STRONG** — Feature #7 |
| **Rooftop PV subsystem** on terminal buildings | LOW — Mein Solar incumbent | site-dependent, 1–5 MWp typical | **STRONG** — core business |
| **Ferry Megawatt Charging subsystem** (MCS 3 MW+) | HIGH — new standard, new connector | €100 M+ per major ferry port to 2030 | WEAK near-term; STRONG by 2028 |
| **Wind-energy subsystem** (small-scale port wind) | HIGH — turbine procurement, structural | site-dependent | WEAK |

### Vertical Up — L5 → L6 move (Terminal-as-a-Service)

| Factor | Evaluation |
|---|---|
| Additional capabilities | O&M, power trading, asset ownership, customer-facing SLA |
| Capital investment | HIGH — asset-ownership IPP balance-sheet shift |
| Existing customer leverage | MEDIUM — port authorities buy capex today, not -as-a-Service |
| Competitive response risk | HIGH — utilities (E.ON, EnBW) defend this terrain |
| Time to market | 18–24 months with financial partner |

### Vertical Down / Cross-System — Same L4 subsystems, different end-use

| Target System | BOM Position | Shared Technology | Adaptation Needed |
|---|---|---|---|
| Data-center hybrid UPS+BESS (NAICS 518210) | L5 end product | Full BESS L4.1–4.5 | Higher availability class; tighter cybersec |
| HPC fuel-station BESS (NAICS 457120) | L5 end product | Full BESS + L3.2 PCS | Smaller form-factor; DC-coupled HPC interface |
| Industrial microgrid (C&I) | L5 end product | BESS + grid coupling | Islanding / black-start controls |
| PV+BESS solar park (NAICS 221114) | L5 end product | Full stack | Incumbent — no adaptation |

---

sections.title: Quality Check
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Every L4 has ≥1 L3 | ✅ (6/6) |
| Every L3 has ≥1 L2 | ✅ |
| Every L2 traces to ≥1 L1 | ✅ |
| Every L1 traces to ≥1 L0 | ✅ |
| Alternatives table at every level transition (incl. single-source) | ✅ |
| Shares sum to 100% ±1% | ✅ |
| Confidence + source per row | ✅ |
| Packaging/containment L4 included | ✅ (L4.5) |
| Control/interface L4 included | ✅ (L4.4) |
| Company position marked | ✅ (L5 end product) |
| Horizontal + vertical adjacency (B.6) | ✅ |
| BOM vs VN boundary respected | ✅ (all items END UP IN asset, not items that MAKE it) |

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- [Wood Mackenzie — LFP to overtake NMC as dominant stationary storage chemistry by 2030](https://www.woodmac.com/press-releases/lfp-to-overtake-nmc-as-dominant-stationary-storage-chemistry-by-2030/)
- [BloombergNEF — Global Energy Storage Growth Upheld by New Markets](https://about.bnef.com/insights/clean-energy/global-energy-storage-growth-upheld-by-new-markets/)
- [IEA — Global battery markets are growing strongly](https://www.iea.org/commentaries/global-battery-markets-are-growing-strongly-and-so-are-the-supply-risks)
- [IDTechEx — Batteries for Stationary Energy Storage 2025-2035](https://www.idtechex.com/en/research-report/batteries-for-stationary-energy-storage/1021)
- [Fortune Business Insights — Medium Voltage Switchgear Market 2025-2032](https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/industry-reports/medium-voltage-switchgear-market-100927)
- [Mordor Intelligence — Medium Voltage Switchgear Market to 2031](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/medium-voltage-switchgear-market)
- [Verified Market Research — Gas Insulated Switchgear SF6-Free Market](https://www.verifiedmarketresearch.com/product/gas-insulated-switchgear-sf6-free-market/)
- [BIS Research — Liquid Cooling Market for Stationary BESS](https://bisresearch.com/industry-report/liquid-cooling-market-for-stationary-battery-energy-storage-system.html)
- [Business Research Insights — Energy Storage PCS Market to 2033](https://www.businessresearchinsights.com/market-reports/energy-storage-power-conversion-system-pcs-market-121566)
- [Interact Analysis — Power Conversion System revenues to reach $12.7bn by 2029](https://interactanalysis.com/power-conversion-system-revenues-to-reach-12-7bn-by-2029/)
- [US DOE — BESS Supply Chain Battery Report Nov 2024](https://www.energy.gov/sites/default/files/2025-01/BESSIE_supply-chain-battery-report_111124_OPENRELEASE_SJ_1.pdf)
- [NREL ATB 2024 — Utility-Scale Battery Storage](https://atb.nrel.gov/electricity/2024/utility-scale_battery_storage)
- [IBISWorld — NAICS 488310 Port and Harbor Operations](https://www.ibisworld.com/classifications/naics/488310/port-and-harbor-operations/)
- [ScienceDirect — Coordinated energy and logistics scheduling for electrified seaports](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0306261925013753)

rows.Port-specific note: Sizing for crane regen + ferry peak pulses; LFP preferred for safety near fuel & vessels
Low-frequency output (60 Hz→50/60 Hz converter) for shore power via add-on stage

rows.Function: Couple to 10/20 kV DSO + internal port MV ring
rows.Port-specific note: Ring feed to shore-power connection points; OPS IEC/IEEE 80005-1 interface
Integrated with port TOS (Terminal Operating System) for crane load forecast
Quay-side seismic/hydrostatic uplift; bollard protection for terminal tractor impact

rows.Alternative: Cell + BMS + Thermal + Fire (integrated module architecture)

rows.Source: CATL EnerC / Sungrow PowerTitan / BYD MC Cube spec sheets

rows.Share: 58% of Li-ion BESS; >90% of new stationary in CN

rows.L1 parts: Ferrite / iron-powder core inductor + PP cap

rows.L1: PCB + TI C2000 / ADI SHARC + FPGA (Xilinx/Intel)

rows.L1 / L0: PCB + ARM SoC; Siemens SIPROTEC 40% / ABB Relion 25% / SEL 20% / Schneider 15%
VDE-AR-N 4110 certified relay (single-source at L2, brand choice at SKU)

rows.Alternative: Independent EMS (Entrix, Next Kraftwerke, Energy Toolbase, GridBeyond)

rows.L0: Portland cement (CEM II 70% / CEM I 25% / CEM III 5%)
Hot-rolled carbon steel B500B (95%) / stainless SS 2205 (5% port splash zone)

rows.L1 / L0: Galvanized steel mesh 80% / palisade 15% / concrete wall 5%

rows.Adjacent L4: **Shore Power Conversion subsystem** (50↔60 Hz, 6.6/11 kV frequency converter)
rows.Capability Gap: MEDIUM — needs AFE + isolation transformer design
rows.Market Size (est.): ~€2 bn EU ports to 2030 (EU AFIR mandate)

rows.Adjacent L4: **HPC Charging subsystem** for port-truck / reach-stacker fleets
rows.Capability Gap: LOW — already in Mein Solar feature set

rows.Market Size (est.): €100 M+ per major ferry port to 2030

rows.Evaluation: O&M, power trading, asset ownership, customer-facing SLA
MEDIUM — port authorities buy capex today, not -as-a-Service
HIGH — utilities (E.ON, EnBW) defend this terrain

rows.Check: Alternatives table at every level transition (incl. single-source)

rows.Status: ✅ (all items END UP IN asset, not items that MAKE it)

entities.bom_applies_because: focal company deliverable is a physical BESS installation; port itself has no manufactured output
port_specific_adaptations: Ester-fluid MV transformer (spill liability near waterfront)

options.name: Independent EMS (Entrix, Next Kraftwerke, Energy Toolbase)

vertical_down_cross_system.adaptation: Smaller form factor, DC-coupled HPC interface
